phase: Phase 0 — scaffolding, packaging boundary, humanize spike
surface: api/package (stdio MCP server + hyper-cloaking-engine workspace package)

P0-a.evidence: artifacts/humanize-spike-matrix.json: engine.humanClick 70 trusted moves; engine.humanType paced (243-266ms) trusted; engine.humanScroll 6 wheel steps. Default engine-routed path proven humanized.

P0-b.evidence: test/import-smoke.test.mjs: mcp imports hyper-cloaking-engine + /action-runtime/guardrails.mjs + /providers/index.mjs + /target-safety.mjs by package name.

P0-c.evidence: test/bundle.test.mjs: engine bundle keeps import("cloakbrowser") dynamic, preserves the runtime workspace-fallback path string, and does not inline cloakbrowser source.

P0-d.evidence: Queue busy-bound < min client tool-call timeout recorded as the Phase-2 session-manager contract; no queue exists yet in Phase 0.

decision: Option A confirmed by measured matrix: page.keyboard.press(raw) produced 0 pointer moves (not humanized) and locator.fill did not pace keystrokes, so engine-routed input stays the shipped surface; un-proven raw paths are refused at the Phase-2 tool boundary.

adversarialCases.scenario: call a non-existent tool over stdio
adversarialCases.expected: structured isError result, never a raw throw crossing the MCP boundary
adversarialCases.evidence: test/server.stdio.test.mjs: result.isError === true, message matches /Unknown tool/

adversarialCases.scenario: drive raw page.keyboard.press on a humanize:true context and measure motion
adversarialCases.expected: measurably NOT humanized (proves the verified gap, justifying engine-routed default)

adversarialCases.scenario: attempt to detect cloakbrowser source inlined into the distribution bundle

artifactRefs.description: per-input-method behavioral humanization matrix from a real CloakBrowser context

regressions: none — 374 engine unit tests + 5 e2e unchanged and green; validate + engine:validate green after mirroring engine/package.json into all four skill roots.
